title: Sentimental Value
logline: After her mother's death, an actress struggles with family dysfunction and her own identity while her documentary filmmaker father tries to hold onto their ancestral home and his career through an affair with a famous actress.
antagonistOrConflict: Family dysfunction and the weight of inherited trauma across generations
tone: Melancholic, introspective, with moments of dark humor

actBreaks.description: Nora and Gustav's relationship shifts from funeral politeness to deeper conflict during their pub conversation where past grievances surface.

validation.ruling: Scene 74 at the Lorry Pub where Gustav and Nora have their first real conversation since the funeral represents the major shift from the funeral/family crisis setup into the new dynamic of father-daughter relationship exploration and Gustav's film project revelation.

actBreaks.description: After Gustav's emotional breakdown and Rachel's departure, the family dynamic fundamentally changes as they confront selling the house.

validation.ruling: Scene 139 where Rachel visits Agnes to discuss Gustav's mother and the family secrets represents a crucial turning point that shifts the story from the film preparation phase into the final act of personal reckonings and revelations.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated temporal weaving structure, constantly shifting between different time periods within the family house to reveal how past traumas echo through generations. The house itself functions as both narrator and central character, with scenes flowing organically between decades to show how emotional patterns repeat. Rather than following a traditional three-act structure, the story builds through emotional revelations and family confrontations, with the house serving as the constant that connects all temporal threads. The non-linear approach allows the writers to explore how memory, trauma, and family dynamics layer upon each other across time.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing alternates between intimate character moments and sweeping historical montages. The opening house narration (scenes 1-19) establishes the temporal fluidity that continues throughout. Key emotional beats are given breathing room through longer dialogue scenes (like scene 74 at the pub), while transitional moments use rapid cutting between time periods to show emotional connections across decades.

incitingIncident.description: Nora enters a room to find her dead mother Sissel lying motionless in bed, touches her hand gently, then another woman appears in the doorway.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. NURSING HOME / MOTHER'S ROOM - EVENING

validation.ruling: Scene 39 where Nora discovers her mother Sissel dead in the nursing home bed and embraces her crying sister Agnes shatters Nora's ordinary world and establishes the irreversible stakes that will drive the entire story forward.

midpoint.description: Rachel meets Agnes in the family house, with Agnes barely able to speak from nerves, and Rachel learning that Agnes played Anna in Gustav's earlier film.

validation.ruling: Scene 119 is too passive for a true midpoint. Scene 111 at the family house represents Nora's shift from reactive to proactive behavior as she takes decisive action regarding her relationship with Gustav and confronts the family dynamics that have been building throughout the first half.

climax.description: Sitting in the empty auditorium, Rachel asks why Nora didn't want the film role, and Nora explains she can't work with Gustav because they can't talk, while Rachel reveals she's thinking of dropping out because she can't understand the character.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ MAIN STAGE - EVENING

validation.ruling: Scene 181 shows Gustav's crisis but lacks direct protagonist confrontation. Scene 180 provides the true climax where Nora and Rachel sit in the empty auditorium having their ultimate confrontation about the film role, family dynamics, and character understanding - this is Nora's peak moment of truth and resolution.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (PRESENT, WINTER) - DAY
scenes.summary: A worn-down family house in Oslo stands in need of paint among more modern neighboring homes.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (APPROX. 2000, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: The same house appears in summer circa 2000 with fresh paint as the narrator explains a girl's essay assignment about being an object.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1990S) - DAY
scenes.summary: Two girls run down the stairs and out the door in the 1990s as the narrator describes the house's perspective on their movement.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1990S) - DAY
scenes.summary: The two girls squeeze through a fence opening and run off while the narrator discusses the house's ability to see behind itself.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (VARIOUS TIMES) - DAY
scenes.summary: A montage shows the house being used over decades with scratches and wear as the narrator wonders if the house prefers emptiness or fullness.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1990S) - DAY
scenes.summary: A crack appears in the house's foundation as the narrator explains the father's comment about an old structural flaw.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1990S) - DAY
scenes.summary: The foundation crack is visible inside the basement and extends up through the library to the ceiling.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (APPROX. 2000)
scenes.summary: Twelve-year-old Nora covers her head with a pillow to block out her parents' loud argument as the narrator describes the house's slow collapse.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1918, FALL) - DAY
scenes.summary: A dead man lies in the third-floor bedroom with flowers on his bedspread as the narrator identifies him as Nora's great-grandfather Edvard who died of Spanish flu in 1918.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1925, WINT ER) - DAY
scenes.summary: A midwife places a newborn baby in the mother's arms in the same bedroom where Edvard died seven years earlier.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (APPROX. 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: Sissel and Gustav argue fiercely in the bedroom circa 2000 with the sound heavily muted.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (CA 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Nora holds a pillow over her head in her childhood room while her parents' muffled argument continues.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1955, SPRING) - DAY
scenes.summary: Otto drops an orange that rolls away due to the slanted floor before he freezes and his body hits the floor, dying of a heart attack in 1955.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1958) - DAY
scenes.summary: Karin sits in the living room with her head down as the narrator reveals she hanged herself in 1958 and the room darkens when a cloud covers the sun.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1985) - DAY
scenes.summary: The narrator states that Edith died in the same bedroom where she was born, thirty years after Karin's suicide.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (2008) - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora's former childhood room is now used for storage as the narrator reflects on teenage Nora's essay about family 'noise' rather than 'quarreling.'

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (QUICK MONTAGE, VARIOUS YEARS & SEASONS)
scenes.summary: Gustav passes by the house's window on multiple occasions across different years as the narrator describes the house preferring noise to silence.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (APPROX. 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: Sissel sits melancholically in the kitchen while twelve-year-old Nora watches her as the narrator explains how the house missed Gustav's sounds after he left.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE, NORA’S CHILDHOOD ROOM (2008) - DAY
scenes.summary: Twenty-year-old Nora reads her old essay in her cluttered childhood room and practices Nina's monologue from 'The Seagull' for theatre auditions.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, STAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Thirty-five-year-old Nora breathes nervously backstage at the National Theatre while looking out at the expectant audience before her performance.

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL THEATRE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Theatre audience members walk through snow-covered streets toward the National Theatre for the premiere performance.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, LOBBY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Theatre patrons check coats and enter the main hall while ushers distribute programs for the performance.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, MAIN HALL & BEHIND STAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hundreds of audience members fill the theatre seats while Nora hides in darkness at the back of the stage, breathing frantically with fear.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, DRESSING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora retreats to her dressing room in apparent panic and refuses to come out when the stage manager knocks and calls for her.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, AUDITORIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The stage manager signals the director from the auditorium that something is wrong and he gets up to address the situation.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, DRESSING ROOMS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The director tries to calm Nora at her dressing room door, telling her to use her anxiety on stage, while she insists this time is different.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora gets miked up by the sound technician after the director successfully persuades her to perform.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, AUDITORIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The director returns to his seat next to his husband as the theatre lights begin to dim for the performance.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora stands with other actors ready to go on stage but signals to Jakob and backs away toward the hallway exit, telling the stage manager they need to rehearse something.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora runs down the hallway tearing at her costume and hair while the stage manager and costume supervisor chase after her to prevent damage.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora's complete breakdown threatens to derail the entire performance

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, M AIN AUDITORIUM & BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The confused audience waits in the darkened theatre while backstage crew physically restrain Nora and frantically repair her costume with duct tape before she finally goes on stage.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, DRESSING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora's empty dressing room sits undisturbed while her phone vibrates on the sofa.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE / BACKSTAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora exits the stage with other actors to hugs and congratulations, receiving flowers and sharing them with the stage technician who had restrained her.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE, DRESS ING ROOM
scenes.summary: Nora's phone continues vibrating alarmingly in her empty dressing room.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora sits in a taxi removing her stage makeup with wet wipes, appearing clearly stressed.

scenes.heading: EXT. NURSING HOME PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The taxi drives into a parking lot in front of a large hospital-like building.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI IN THE NURSING HOME PARKING LOT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora pays the taxi driver but remains seated with her hand on the door handle, asking to sit for a moment while he looks at her with concern.

scenes.heading: INT. NURSING HOME - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora walks through nursing home corridors and slows down as she approaches a specific room.

scenes.heading: INT. NURSING HOME / MOTHER'S ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora enters a room to find her dead mother Sissel lying motionless in bed, touches her hand gently, then another woman appears in the doorway.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora discovers her mother has died - a devastating loss that will reshape the story

scenes.heading: EXT. THE ROAD OUTSIDE THE FAMILY HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav, a well-known but aging film director, steps out of a taxi in black clothing at the family house, looking tired and worn on what is clearly not a good day.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE/KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes and Nora prepare food in the kitchen for their mother's funeral reception, both stressed and dressed in black while guests murmur in other rooms.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOME, LIVING ROOM/ KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav enters the house full of funeral guests and retreats to the library to avoid being seen by Nora before she can notice him.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: The house suddenly empties of all funeral guests and becomes completely quiet as faint music plays from far away.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1930S, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: The same room appears in the 1930s summer where twelve-year-old Karin enjoys music with friends on a hand-cranked record player.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1930S) - DAY
scenes.summary: Ten-year-old Edith lies by the fireplace in Nora's room listening to music and giggling from Karin and her friends below.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1940S) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Seventeen-year-old Edith moves closer to the fireplace to listen to indistinct whispered discussion from the room below before music drowns out the voices.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1940S, FALL) - DAY
scenes.summary: Twenty-year-old Karin in a coat walks out of the gate as seen from a first-floor window in the 1940s.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1943, WINTER) - EVENING
scenes.summary: State Police in Nazi-style uniforms escort Karin from the house to a police car in 1943 while she glances back at the second-floor window.
scenes.turningPointNote: Karin's arrest by Nazi collaborators represents a major trauma in the family's generational history

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1 944, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: Karin's room sits mostly empty with some belongings remaining after she fled to Sweden following her release from imprisonment.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1944, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: The first-floor library sits abandoned with the unused record player after Karin's departure.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (PRESENT DAY, WINTER) - DAY
scenes.summary: During the funeral reception, Gustav sits thoughtfully in Sissel's office while the narrator reveals that Karin returned to Norway as Karin Borg after the liberation, married to Lennart.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1950S, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: Three-year-old Gustav runs happily through the house in the 1950s, playing with spilled milk on the table and standing on the porch in the rain.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1950S, VARIOUS SEASONS) - DAY
scenes.summary: A montage shows young Gustav's love for the magical light patterns created by sun and shadows throughout the family house across various seasons.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1958) - DAY
scenes.summary: Karin sits in the darkened living room, then walks down the hallway and closes herself in the room at the end as the narrator reveals she died and Lennart took Gustav to Sweden.
scenes.turningPointNote: Karin's death fundamentally changes the family structure and Gustav's life trajectory

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1965) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Years later, the closed door opens to reveal jovial young women emerging into a living room full of party guests as Edith and Lillian take over the house.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (1965) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The house's first floor windows are all lit up with the full party visible inside.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1965) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: From inside the party, lights turn on in neighboring windows as the narrator reveals Edith's suspicion that a neighbor had informed on Karin during the war.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 1965, SUMMER) - DAY
scenes.summary: Thirteen-year-old Gustav talks with Aunt Edith while she hangs laundry, as the narrator recounts her advice about sexual freedom, while Gustav glances at the sunbathing Lillian.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 1965, SUMMER) - SUNSET
scenes.summary: At sunset, Edith and Lillian sit smoking on the terrace steps with Lillian's head on Edith's shoulder as the narrator reveals Gustav inherited the house when Edith died.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1986, FALL) - DAY
scenes.summary: The family house entrance and living room appear empty and worn down, having lost its charm since the happy 1960s.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1986, FALL) - DAY
scenes.summary: Thirty-five-year-old Gustav paints walls in the second-floor bedroom, having moved back to Norway and the house with beautiful light.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (1986, FALL) - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gustav sits motionless with painting equipment, displaying his mother's melancholy as he looks toward the room where she died, while the narrator explains Sissel's version of how they met.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 2000) - EVENING
scenes.summary: On a TV talk show, fifty-year-old Gustav tells his exaggerated version of meeting Sissel, claiming he pretended to have psychological problems to see the beautiful psychologist.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (EARLY 90S) - DAY
scenes.summary: Three-year-old Nora runs through the modernized house rooms and out to the veranda as the narrator explains Gustav and Sissel's marriage and the birth of their daughters.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (MONTAGE, 1988 - 2020)
scenes.summary: A montage of family memories layers together, from Gustav showing baby Nora the moon to a plate shattering on the floor during what appears to be a domestic argument.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 2020) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lights are extinguished one by one in the family house until only one window remains lit, with a frail female figure walking alone inside as the narrator explains the daughters moving out.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 2020) - DAY
scenes.summary: Household items are misplaced in the fridge, reminder notes are stuck everywhere, a kettle has melted on the stove, and Sissel sits crying while adult Agnes comforts her.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (PRESENT DAY) - DAY
scenes.summary: Back in the present, the living room is full of funeral guests as Nora leads Erik toward the hallway, explaining that grandma used to work in her office helping people.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE/STAIRS TO 2ND FLOOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and Erik walk upstairs as she explains how Sissel talked to people about secret things in her therapy practice.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: In Nora's old bedroom, now filled with clutter, she opens the fireplace and tells Erik how she secretly listened here after school, and they hear Gustav's voice coming through the chimney.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora tells Agnes that their father is at the funeral, and they approach Gustav who is socializing with guests by the fireplace, leading to an awkward family reunion and hug.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: As the funeral reception winds down, Gustav enters through the back door carrying large speakers, explaining to a questioning Nora that he's taking some of his things.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The sisters clean up alone while drinking wine, discussing Gustav's sudden parental behavior and Agnes revealing that he technically still owns the house since no papers were filed during the divorce.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation about house ownership creates a new major conflict about the family's security and future

scenes.heading: INT. LORRY PUB - DAY
scenes.summary: At a traditional Oslo pub, Gustav and Nora have a more relaxed father-daughter meeting where he flirts with the server, expresses worry about Nora, and discusses his life and filmmaking while she tries to steer the conversation away from herself.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CAR IN DEAUVILLE, FRANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: In Deauville, France, Gustav sits in a car with nervous film journalist Christophe, learning that his old contact Jean-Baptiste died of mouth cancer four years ago.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONGRESS CENTER, DEAUVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: At the Deauville Congress Center, Christophe escorts Gustav past photographers and crowds focused on bigger celebrities, though a French film nerd recognizes Gustav and asks for an autograph.

scenes.heading: INT. CONGRESS C ENTER, DEAUVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav and Christophe walk past a long line toward a smaller cinema while the main theater shows an American film starring Rachel Kemp, a charismatic young star who signs autographs before being escorted away by her entourage.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALLER CINEMA, DEAUVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: On stage at the smaller cinema, Gustav does a Q&A about his film retrospective, making jokes about nudity in his early work and low attendance at his later films while promoting his newest premiere.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. TRAIN COMPARTMENT 40S - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav's new film shows on screen: a scene of two children being chased by German soldiers near a train, with Agnes playing the girl Anna convincingly scared with tears in her eyes.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL CINEMA HALL, DEAUVILLE - DAY
scenes.summary: After the film screening, Rachel Kemp sits with tears in her eyes while Gustav stands melancholically by the stage, then participates in a Q&A where he gives a vulnerable response about a rock just being a rock.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH PROMENADE IN DEAUVILLE - JUST BEFORE SUNSET
scenes.summary: Gustav walks alone along the Deauville beach promenade at sunset, passing beach huts with famous names and festival banners featuring Agnes as Anna, until Christophe calls out to him.

scenes.heading: EXT. FANCY FRENCH BRASSERIE BY THE BEACH - EVENING
scenes.summary: Outside a fancy beachside brasserie at evening, paparazzi and fans wait behind barriers hoping to glimpse Rachel Kemp as she leaves.

scenes.heading: INT. FANCY FRENCH BRASSERIE BY THE BEACH - EVENING
scenes.summary: Inside the restaurant, Gustav approaches Rachel's table but is stopped by her bodyguard until Rachel recognizes him and invites him to sit, praising his film as a masterpiece and discussing it with her entourage.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEAUVILLE BEACH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav, Rachel, and her manager walk on the beach at night with champagne, finding it amusing to sneak away while her bodyguard and publicist follow behind.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEAUVILLE BEACH - DA WN
scenes.summary: At dawn on the beach, while Sam sleeps and Nicky dozes, Gustav and Rachel walk in the sand discussing her desire to take a career break, with Gustav encouraging her to say no to projects and live her own life.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEAUVILLE BEACH (2024, SP) - SUNRISE
scenes.summary: At sunrise, Rachel lies under jackets while she and Gustav discuss their difficult relationships with their fathers, with Gustav complimenting her expressive face and casting abilities.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN’S APARTMENT - EVENING
scenes.summary: In Agnes and Even's apartment, they watch Gustav's English-language documentary about Edvald, a supposedly last-of-his-species Amazonian frog, while discussing why there's no Swedish version.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT, ERIK’S ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Erik lies in bed asking Nora about having a boyfriend, then tells her he loves her and wants to marry her when he grows up, which she responds to sweetly without making fun.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT, LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Even, Agnes, and Nora drink wine in the living room, joking about Erik's proposal to Nora, but Agnes notices Nora has tears in her eyes.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora arrives home to her practical but uncozy two-room apartment and sits alone at the kitchen table in semi-darkness, creating a lonely feeling.

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL THEATRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora walks down a street and enters the side entrance of the National Theatre.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Theatre Director confronts Nora about her performance anxiety affecting other actors, then mentions that Olivia has requested her for a lead role in a new production.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTM ENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora lies naked in bed with married actor Jakob after sex, discussing the Theatre Director's concerns and her resistance to therapy, explaining she's attracted to Jakob precisely because their relationship can't become serious.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: In the family house twenty years ago, a therapy conversation can be faintly heard from behind the closed door of Sissel's office.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE/ SISSEL'S OFFICE (CIRCA 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: Inside Sissel's office, therapy sessions with different clients blend together - a woman discussing her husband's ridicule of her guitar playing and a man talking about spending time locked in bathrooms.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE/ NORA'S CHILDHOOD ROOM (CIRCA 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: Twelve-year-old Nora sits by the fireplace in her childhood room, listening to therapy sessions from below through the chimney, hearing a client discuss fear of relationships and Stockholm syndrome.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ REHEARSAL SPACE (PRESENT DAY) - DAY
scenes.summary: In present-day rehearsal, Nora analyzes a scene by comparing the character to her sister Agnes, discussing how their father's abandonment affected Agnes's relationship choices and conflict avoidance.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - EVENING
scenes.summary: At Agnes and Even's apartment, Nora's rehearsal voice continues in voiceover while Agnes asks her what she actually enjoys about acting despite the costs.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ REHEARSAL SPACE - DAY
scenes.summary: After rehearsal, Nora and Jakob wait until they're alone, then he closes the door as she looks at him with clear attraction.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE (CIRCA 2000) - DAY
scenes.summary: Twelve-year-old Nora arrives home from school, walking past her mother's office where therapy is in session, then discovering Gustav and Agnes rehearsing the film Anna with a young assistant in the kitchen.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS IN OSLO - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora walks through Oslo streets while her voiceover discusses acting as escaping herself, and she's visually replaced by other women of different ages.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora sits alone in her bed reading and making notes in her manuscript.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUR SAVIOR'S CEMETERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora jogs intensely through a cemetery alone.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora sits in darkness illuminated only by her phone screen, laughing at something while scrolling in her empty apartment.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS IN OSLO - DAY
scenes.summary: A woman with a red bike sits down to lock it and tears form in her eyes.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: A 50-year-old woman sits at Nora's kitchen table in darkness, then is replaced by other women until finally Nora appears in the chair.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora walks up to the family house where Agnes waits on the terrace, and they hug and smile with Nora appearing happy and unaffected.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and Agnes sort through their mother's packed belongings, with Agnes wanting to preserve items with sentimental value while Nora wants to discard everything, leading to tension over a vase.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora hurries out the back of the house through a hole in the fence, still carrying the vase in her hand.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav opens the door for Rachel but blocks his agent and Nicky, directing them to a café while closing the door.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Rachel meets Agnes in the family house, with Agnes barely able to speak from nerves, and Rachel learning that Agnes played Anna in Gustav's earlier film.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEDIEVAL CASTLE (TV SERIES SHOOTING) - DAY
scenes.summary: Michael excitedly calls Gustav from a medieval castle set to announce that Rachel is confirmed for the film.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gustav sits on the couch trimming his toenails while on speakerphone with Michael, confidently responding to the news about Rachel and turning up New Order music.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Rachel Kemp fans gather with posters at a downtown Oslo hotel entrance hoping to see their idol.

scenes.heading: INT. DOWNTOWN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav participates in a hotel press day with Rachel and other actors, insisting to Michael that cinematographer Peter must work on the film despite Michael's objections about Peter's age.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora eats cereal while listening to audio from the press day on her phone, including Gustav's profanity-laden comments.

scenes.heading: INT. TUBE STATION (2024, SU) - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora rides escalators in a tube station and encounters Rachel Kemp's face on digital billboards, with the two seeming to make eye contact across the screens.

scenes.heading: EXT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora approaches Agnes and Even's apartment building carrying a gift, appearing slightly nervous as she rings the doorbell.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APA RTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: At Erik's 9th birthday party, Gustav arrives with energy and charm, bonding with Nora over teasing Even while Agnes notices their alliance, and Gustav flirts with Therese.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gustav and Nora's unexpected bonding shifts their relationship dynamic

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT/ ERIK'S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Erik sits at his computer using an AI website to create something related to Gustav Borg's filmmaking style, referencing DVDs he received.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT/ LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav criticizes Erik's screen time and makes pointed comments about Even being on his phone, with Nora joining in the mockery while Agnes notices their bonding.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT/ BALCONY - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gustav and Nora smoke together on the balcony in a light mood, with Gustav joking about Therese liking him and Nora warning him not to sleep with her.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - EVENING
scenes.summary: The evening continues with Gustav telling Nora she should write instead of waiting for acting roles, comparing her situation to Rachel's production company while discussing the challenges of being an actress.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS IN OSLO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora walks pensively through deserted Oslo streets at night as a lone car passes by and disappears.

scenes.heading: EXT. OTHER STREETS IN OSLO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav walks alone on the road at night, looking troubled.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav enters the dark family house and sits exhausted in the hallway to remove his shoes as light from a passing car sweeps through the room.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora arrives home and sits in her living room with a beer, ignoring Agnes's phone call and letting it ring until it becomes silent.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora sits on the tram looking tired.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ REHEARSAL SPACE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora stands alone on the empty theater stage and breaks down crying desperately, falling to her knees and then lying on the floor while colleagues watch from the audience.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora's emotional breakdown represents a psychological crisis point

scenes.heading: INT. CAR SWEDISH COUNTRYSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav sits in a car driving through vast Swedish landscapes that remind of his film "Anna."

scenes.heading: EXT. BEAUTIFUL HOUSE BY THE SWEDISH COAST - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav arrives at Peter's beautiful coastal house and finds his cinematographer friend sleeping in a sunchair with Gustav's script on his lap, and they greet each other warmly.

scenes.heading: INT. BEAUTIFUL HOUSE BY THE SWEDISH COAST - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav watches Peter's shaking hands pour whiskey and realizes Michael was right about Peter being too old, despite Peter's insistence he can still work.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gustav confronts the reality that his longtime collaborator is no longer capable

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav sits alone drinking wine in the family house, gets progressively drunk, and notices the kitchen door frame marked with Nora and Agnes's childhood height measurements.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav sits melancholically on the empty bed frame in what was his and Sissel's bedroom, now stripped bare.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav lies drunk on the living room couch calling Nora's voicemail, confessing he misses her and has things to say but can't find the moment because she's always angry with him.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav wakes up hungover on the couch and frantically cleans up evidence of his drinking binge, wiping surfaces and throwing away bottles and glasses.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav works energetically with Rachel and Ingrid on script readings in the family house kitchen, focusing intently on translating dialogue from Norwegian to English.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: During a break in the garden, Rachel questions whether filming in English works and asks Gustav about her character's motivation for suicide, while Gustav deflects her questions.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN’S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Rachel visits Agnes seeking information about Gustav's mother's suicide, learning she was in prison during the war, while Agnes admits she should know more about the family history.

scenes.heading: INT. RACHEL’S HOTEL SUITE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rachel sits alone in her hotel suite practicing a Scandinavian accent and later looks at Nora's Instagram pictures on her phone.

scenes.heading: EXT. OSLO FERRY - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora stands on a ferry deck with director Olivia, Jakob, and four actor colleagues from their new play.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRESSHOLMEN ISLAND - DAY
scenes.summary: The theater group picnics on an Oslo fjord island where Olivia makes a toast, Nora learns Jakob is having troubles at home, and later Nora asks Jakob privately if their relationship is the cause.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRESSHOLMEN ISLAND/ON THE WAY TO THE FERRY - DAY
scenes.summary: Walking to the ferry, Nora tries to kiss Jakob twice but he pulls away, telling her he doesn't know what he wants while avoiding her hurt gaze.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jakob's rejection marks a clear shift in their relationship

scenes.heading: EXT. OSLO FERRY - SUNSET
scenes.summary: On the ferry back to the city, Jakob talks with Olivia while Nora sits alone, and when their eyes meet Jakob looks away.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav takes Rachel aside during script reading break to tell her she seems distracted and advises her to listen and not rush, while Rachel appears nervous.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav and Ingrid lie in bed after sex discussing how young people don't have sex like their generation did, with Ingrid commenting she's glad Gustav saw her naked when they were young.

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES - DAY
scenes.summary: A functionalist building from the 70s near the Oslo forest is shown - the National Archives.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES (MONTAGE) - DAY
scenes.summary: Montage of the National Archives interior showing concrete halls, corridors, and an archivist accessing State Police documents from shelves labeled with official codes.

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes enters the main door of the National Archives building.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes enters the archives building and goes up a staircase.
scenes.turningPointNote: Agnes's research into family history begins in earnest

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES/RECEPTION - DAY
scenes.summary: An archivist at the National Archives presents Agnes with her grandmother's registration card from State Police archives, her entry from Grini prison, and a thick folder containing the treason trial case against Ole Wilhelmsen.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL ARCHIVES/READING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes reads through the archive materials, examining her grandmother Karin Irgens' registration card showing her imprisonment for spreading anti-Nazi propaganda and finding Karin's testimony against torturer Ole Wilhelmsen in a folder filled with hundreds of similar testimonies.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes sits at home with Even while the narrator's voice reflects on what made such a strong impression from her grandmother's unsentimental testimony, interspersed with quick cuts of the torture testimonies and police reports.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - DAY - CLIP FROM FATHER'S FILM ANNA:
scenes.summary: A dark-haired boy from Gustav's film ANNA runs through a rain-soaked forest, reaches a clearing with a large stone, sits exhausted next to it, and lies down as if embraced by the stone.

scenes.heading: INT. OUTDOOR AREA BY AGNES & EVEN’S APT. - DAY
scenes.summary: Gustav demonstrates a death scene for Erik's filming, dramatically falling and spasming as if impaled by a plastic shovel, then gets up gleefully asking how it looked, prompting Agnes to give him a surprised small hug.

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL THEATRE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Documentary-style images show the audience arriving at the National Theatre for Nora's Hamlet performance, with a large poster visible on the theatre wall.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Director Olivia sits nervously with her family in the full theatre hall while Agnes and Even occupy seats with one vacant seat next to Agnes, and the theatre manager talks with someone in the audience.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/DRESSING ROOMS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora warms up in her dressing room with tongue twisters, appearing calm, when the stage manager knocks to announce ten minutes until curtain.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/BACKSTAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora gets fitted with her microphone backstage while other actors cast nervous glances at her, and the stage manager watches her hawkishly as she walks toward the stage edge then turns abruptly the other way.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/MAIN STAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gustav stands hidden at the back of the theatre hall watching Nora perform as Hamlet, becomes visibly moved by something, then suddenly leaves.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/FOYER - EVENING
scenes.summary: Gustav crosses the empty theatre foyer with the sound of the play echoing faintly and exits the theatre.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/MAIN STAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: The play ends to applause and a standing ovation, with Nora receiving an especially large swell of applause as she bows.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ACTORS' FOYER - EVENING
scenes.summary: In the actors' foyer celebration, Agnes and Even arrive and approach Nora, with Agnes noticing something seems off about Nora despite her smiles, and when Agnes asks if she's alright, Nora deflects and hasn't been answering her phone.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/DRESSING ROOMS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora sits alone in her dressing room.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ACTORS' FOYER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nora walks past the celebration room where Olivia, Jakob and others are drinking and laughing late into the night, and continues walking away.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAWN
scenes.summary: Nora sits quietly in her dark apartment at dawn, still wearing the same clothes from after the performance, having been awake all night.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora lies motionless on her living room floor, staring blankly ahead at dust under the sofa.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora sits at her kitchen table making a phone call in a flat, drained voice, saying she doesn't know if she'll be better tomorrow and can't make it tonight, then hangs up and sits motionless before slowly walking to her bedroom as the room darkens.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nora's mental state deteriorates significantly after her successful performance, showing the depth of her depression.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes confronts Gustav about missing Nora's premiere, and when he brings scripts for Erik's film role, Agnes firmly tells him Erik won't be doing the film and that he needs to start putting his children first, causing Gustav to leave hurt.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - EVENING
scenes.summary: Agnes tidies her apartment feeling lighter after confronting her father, then notices Gustav's forgotten script and picks it up to examine it.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Agnes sits on the toilet reading her father's script, clearly captivated by it.

scenes.heading: INT. AGNES AND EVEN'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Agnes finishes reading the script in bed next to sleeping Even, puts it away thoughtfully.

scenes.heading: EXT. NORA'S APARTMENT BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes stands outside Nora's apartment building with the script, ringing the bell persistently and looking up at the windows when there's no immediate answer.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA'S APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes enters Nora's dark apartment with blinds down and untouched takeaway food, finding Nora unkempt and unresponsive, then shows her Gustav's script about their grandmother and convinces her to read it.

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL THEATRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora enters the theatre through the side entrance for another day's performance.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/DRE SSING ROOMS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora sits in her dressing room with noise and conversations outside.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/MAIN STAGE AND AUDITORIUM - EVENING
scenes.summary: After the performance, the excited cast comes off stage to continuing applause and returns to bow again, with Nora spotting Rachel in the audience clapping.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/FOYER - EVENING
scenes.summary: Nora meets Rachel by the backstage entrance, both wearing similar clothes and hairstyles, and they smile at noticing their resemblance.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/BACKSTAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rachel compliments Nora's performance as they move through the backstage toward the empty stage, where Rachel stands in the middle looking out at the auditorium with reverence.

scenes.heading: INT. NATIONAL THEATRE/ MAIN STAGE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Sitting in the empty auditorium, Rachel asks why Nora didn't want the film role, and Nora explains she can't work with Gustav because they can't talk, while Rachel reveals she's thinking of dropping out because she can't understand the character.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav and Michael drink together in the family house as Gustav admits he's losing control, feels too old, and suggests finding another boy to replace Erik while lamenting his declining career and missing his deceased collaborator Peter.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gustav reaches a breaking point, acknowledging his failures and considering abandoning his grandson's role.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rachel arrives at the family house at night and Gustav greets her from the terrace, offering her tea and warm socks as she comes inside from the cold.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav makes tea for Rachel as she confirms she doesn't want to do the film anymore but doesn't want to let him down, and he responds that most actors would either do the role anyway or just leave through their agents.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: As Gustav helps Rachel with her coat, she glances toward the room where his mother died and says she'll never understand how a mother could do that, before Gustav tells her not to think of this as a failure and waves her away to be great in something else.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - MONTAGE OF DIFFERENT TIMES
scenes.summary: The camera moves continuously through the family house as time shifts between different periods, creating a dreamlike montage with overlapping voices and glimpses of various people who have lived there across the years.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustav stands alone in the living room surrounded by memories, turns on loud music to drown out the oppressive silence, lets his phone ring unanswered, turns the music up uncomfortably loud, then escapes outside into the cold garden without a coat.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - DAWN
scenes.summary: At dawn, Gustav lies motionless on the ground by the garden chair while loud music still plays and all the lights remain on in the house.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gustav collapses outside in the cold, potentially seriously injured or worse.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL/ CORRI DORS - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora walks stressed through hospital corridors, then Agnes reassures her that Gustav got very cold but has been conscious.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL/GUSTAV’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora enters Gustav's quiet hospital room, finds him sleeping and connected to a heart monitor looking frail and disheveled, then sits beside him taking his hand in silence.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL/CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Agnes buys coffee from a machine in the hospital corridor while both sisters seem lost in thought, then Nora unexpectedly hugs Agnes as doctors walk by.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL/CORRIDOR OUTSIDE GUSTAV’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora and Agnes approach Gustav's hospital room hearing his surprisingly energetic voice flirting with a young nurse who brings him juice, and they exchange puzzled looks at his behavior.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FAMILY HOUSE - MONTAGE SALE & RENOVATION
scenes.summary: The family house is sold and undergoes complete renovation, with the familiar wooden planks, wallpaper, kitchen, and fireplace all removed and replaced, transforming it into a typical white modern box.

scenes.heading: INT. FAMILY HOUSE (FILM STUDIO) - DAY
scenes.summary: Nora makes sandwiches in what appears to be the old family kitchen restored as a film set, efficiently packing Erik's lunch including cookies after he mentions other kids get them, showing her practiced routine of making many lunchboxes before.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that this is Nora in the reconstructed family house as a film set suggests a major time jump and role reversal.
